mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE remains below 4% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust manufacturing activity that raises equity earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions, or 60-day returns fall below 1% in 50%+ of co-increasing instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.3 million units indicates strong consumer demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where HOUST exceeds 1.3M for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 2.5% signals central bank policy inaction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days due to rising real interest rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) below 1% growth signals economic weakness that depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of periods where GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment (UNRATE) and increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates labor market overheating, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days due to inflationary pressures.
failure_condition: Interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS shows positive coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions, or 20-day returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of co-moving instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of periods where INDPRO remains below 100 for three consecutive months.
